article_title: Pentagon and Linux Foundation Launch OCUDU, an Open-Source Software Stack Aiming to Become the 'Linux of RAN' for 5G and 6G
summary: Rejected as duplicate. This is the third submission covering the same OCUDU/Pentagon/Linux Foundation launch story. PR #158 holds chronological priority (submitted 2026-03-03) and is pending corrections. PR #164 was already rejected on duplicate grounds.

findings.message: Duplicate story: OCUDU/Pentagon/Linux Foundation launch already covered in PR #158 (chronological priority) and PR #164 (also rejected as duplicate).

content_preview.title: Pentagon and Linux Foundation Launch OCUDU, an Open-Source Software Stack Aiming to Become the 'Linux of RAN' for 5G and 6G
content_preview.summary: The OCUDU Ecosystem Foundation unites 47 organizations under the Linux Foundation to build an open-source radio access network stack backed by the Pentagon's FutureG office, with code due on GitHub in April.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Linux Foundation announced the formation of the Open Centralized Unit Distributed Unit (OCUDU) Ecosystem Foundation at Mobile World Congress 2026 in Barcelona on March 1. The initiative, seeded by the U.S. Department of Defense's FutureG office through the National Spectrum Consortium, aims to produce a production-ready, open-source radio access network (RAN) software stack for 5G and early 6G deployments. The first version of the codebase is scheduled to be published on GitHub ...
